message:
fix(tests,config,serve): repair CI failures across config merge, alerts, pane-identity, bead handlers, and perf bench

Tests and code drifted in ways that only surfaced on CI (empty HOME,
race-enabled runs, fresh macOS workspace). Specifically:

- internal/config/config.go: loadWithCWD() invoked Default() which
  auto-discovered command_palette.md from the ambient process cwd. When
  callers (LoadMerged) pass an explicit cwd, that ambient discovery
  leaked into the final palette. Reset cfg.Palette to hardcoded
  defaults when a cwd is supplied so the subsequent cwd-aware discovery
  step is authoritative. Fixes TestLoadMerged/uses_merged_cwd on CI
  where $HOME has no ~/.config/ntm/command_palette.md to mask the bug.

- internal/alerts/generator.go: checkDependencyCycles emitted a
  slog.Warn when bv was absent; this contaminated stderr which the
  robot-status e2e captures into the same buffer it json.Unmarshals,
  breaking TestSurfaceTruthfulness_RobotStatus and _RobotTerse. Now
  also treats bv.ErrNotInstalled as a silent skip.

- internal/agentmail/pane_identity_test.go: TestCanonicalIdentityPath
  MatchesRustReference and TestRustContractCompatibility relied on
  os.UserConfigDir honouring XDG_CONFIG_HOME — true on Linux, not on
  macOS (it uses $HOME/Library/Application Support). Set HOME too and
  derive the expected base from os.UserConfigDir so both platforms
  agree.

- internal/serve/beads.go: handleCreateBead/Update/Claim/AddBeadDep
  checked bv.IsBdInstalled() before validating the request body,
  returning 503 for syntactically bad requests when bd was absent.
  Reorder so body validation runs first — bad requests get 400
  regardless of tool availability. Fixes TestHandleCreateBead_Invalid
  Body and peers.

- internal/tui/dashboard/bench_test.go: TestViewRenderTime asserted
  <16ms/frame which is meaningless under -race (2–10x slowdown is
  expected). Skip under race via a build-tag-gated raceEnabled
  constant (new race_test.go / norace_test.go).

- internal/cli/init_test.go: TestInstallGitHooks_GitRepo/_Force now
  skip when ntm is not on $PATH (the pre-commit hook generator shells
  out with exec.LookPath). CI does `go build` but does not install
  the binary, so these always fail there.

- internal/ensemble/session_store_test.go: TestSessionStore_Retries
  AfterOpenFailure now also clears XDG_CONFIG_HOME and NTM_CONFIG so
  DefaultPath() truly falls back to the invalid HOME path the test
  constructs.

- internal/checkpoint/branch_test.go and internal/cli/assign_test.go:
  compare paths through filepath.EvalSymlinks so macOS's
  /tmp → /private/tmp and /var/folders → /private/var/folders
  resolution does not break the assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -99,6 +99,11 @@ func TestSessionStore_RetriesAfterOpenFailure(t *testing.T) {
 
 	resetDefaultStateStoreForTest()
 	t.Setenv("HOME", badHome)
+	// Ensure DefaultPath() falls through to HOME instead of using ambient
+	// XDG_CONFIG_HOME / NTM_CONFIG that CI runners may pre-populate with a
+	// valid directory (which would defeat the "invalid HOME path" scenario).
+	t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", "")
+	t.Setenv("NTM_CONFIG", "")
 
 	bad := &EnsembleSession{
 		SessionName:       "bad-open",
